fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer physical therapist license to mississippi, physical therapist compact mississippi, mississippi physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Mississippi Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address: 2478 S. Frontage Road, Suite 300, Vicksburg, MS 39180

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Mississippi.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Access the MSBPT Online Application Portal (msbpt.boardsofnursing.org), register for a new user account if you are a first-time applicant, and then log in to select the 'PT Licensure by Endorsement' application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fees
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within the MSBPT Online Application Portal. Pay the non-refundable $150 application fee and the $75 initial licensure fee directly through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for the submission of all necessary supporting documents, including official transcripts, NPTE scores, verification of current and past licenses from other states (VeriFied by Nursys or direct verification), and a criminal background check authorization form as instructed by the Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided by the Board to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through an approved vendor.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application and submitted documents via the MSBPT Online Application Portal. The Board will review your application once all required documentation has been received.

steps.title: Receive Licensure
steps.description: Upon successful review and approval by the Mississippi State Board of Physical Therapy, your Mississippi Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mississippi participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Mississippi without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Mississippi
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Mississippi, applicants must complete the entire process through the MSBPT Online Application Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MSBPT Online Application Portal, click 'Register' for first-time users, create an account with a valid email, username, and password, then log in to start an application.
